description: vc33 COMPLETE 7/7. 184% efficiency. Dual-button puzzle: brown (instant wall swap) + blue (animated goal transfer). Win condition requires structural alignment, not just position.

TWO BUTTON TYPES: brown squares at edges = instant wall movement. Blue rectangles in middle = animated goal transfer (starts inactive, turns olive when ready). Blue buttons are free — animation doesn't consume move budget.

ENABLER PATTERN: when a button stops working (depleted), clicking a different button that seems to do nothing resets it. Enablers can chain 3+ deep. Try all buttons when stuck, not just the ones that visibly move things.

POSITION != WIN: goal at target position does NOT always clear the level. The game checks which wall section the goal is in, not just where it is. Blue button animations fix this structural mismatch. If position is right but level didn't clear, activate a blue button.

DISCOVERY STRATEGY: spend first ~8 clicks mapping what each button does (click once, observe). Brown buttons come in pairs (forward/reverse). Identify which moves the goal toward the target. Then play strategically, not randomly.
L1: 3 clicks on one brown button. Simplest level — one goal, two buttons, direct path.
L2: forward button depletes after 3 clicks. Use enabler (no visible effect) to refill, then forward again. Pattern: 3F, E, F, E, F.
L3: 3 goals, 8 brown buttons. Some buttons move 2 goals simultaneously. Enabler chain is 3 layers deep. Move goals in priority order.
L4: FIRST LEVEL REQUIRING BLUE BUTTONS. Brown clicks arrange walls until blue turns olive, then click blue for animation. Two blue activations needed.
L5: Most complex. 5 blue-button activations across all 3 blue buttons. 2 goals need structural swaps via blue animations.
L6: 2 blue-button activations. Setup pattern: brown arrange, blue swap, brown forward, blue swap, brown finish.
L7: 3 goals, 4 blue-button activations including 2 back-to-back. Grid is smaller (48x48) with display offset.
